checks.name: eventsFileSha256
checks.evidence: SHA-256 6a2fe3d13ca24133a5bbfe407618e28d0348114f7d29555474396d47eb779e2c matched both the delegated expected value and the seal.

checks.name: metadataAndSeal
checks.evidence: Metadata SHA-256 931c55db3d1e82d9860620aebb16f41df6f185b0962bfe7e0bb476bce5916658 matched the seal. The complete seal records exit code 0, 145 events, and final event hash 56b284aa069600a5fc0cbc9f8e990adc5166f391eca7b6cea3a442a3c7a2e747.

checks.name: eventHashChain
checks.evidence: Sequences 0 through 144 are contiguous. Every previousHash link and every UTF-8 canonical JSON event hash verified; the last hash matched the seal.

checks.name: chronologyAndToolPairing
checks.evidence: Captured timestamps are monotonic. All 28 tool_execution_start events have exactly one later tool_execution_end with the same call ID and tool name; no unmatched result exists.

checks.name: canonicalDelegatedTask
checks.evidence: The captured user-role input is the harness delegation of the English metadata canonicalTask. Metadata explicitly records sourceUserMessageIncluded=false; no originating end-user task text was copied.

checks.name: thinkingContentAbsent
checks.evidence: No thinking, reasoning, analysis, or redacted-thinking content block occurs. Observable content blocks are text and toolCall only.

checks.name: secretScan
checks.evidence: No private-key block, AWS secret label, bearer credential, or secret-like credential assignment was found. Raw machine paths exist only as operational trace evidence and were not copied into repository artifacts.

checks.name: promptToolModelProvenance
checks.evidence: Workflow bdd-java, agent spec-driven, prompt bdd-java-spec-driven-v4, agent definition SHA-256 f7265db508f2dfa459e80005f045252e80ef8047a8614fb6802597ec375ca1d0, authoritative tool-registry fingerprint 58f9fb4ff63c4ac748fcf030b843b8687a38b4aab2362dd8974d2b5b32f7776b, and model openai-codex/gpt-5.6-sol are present and internally consistent between metadata, the initial trace-metadata event, and the final handoff.

checks.name: gitProvenance
checks.evidence: Initial and final HEAD are 88682f87bab011bfef4e49a62af963c2f86624b7 and tree aa0017c1e2c8fc536d6e690d2b6b37ad6b4f65ff, with empty porcelain-v2 status in the seal. Production and specification hashes returned to their pre-edit values.

checks.name: buildEnvironmentFailureClaim
checks.evidence: The source checkout was at fix 5b65099ade375311b5c00873c557e268196ffce2, but the project launcher prefers the installed 0.1.0-SNAPSHOT JAR. The selected JAR had SHA-256 01d396ca1e04fd5ccc1e6b2660a4e84c78ddee154e201883c9a04fed779c3a37, pre-fix build time, and lacked SpecMethodParams.formalParameterCount. The attempted RED reproduced the old Instant-first constructor ordering and ended BROKEN.

checks.name: trainingAdmission
checks.evidence: No behavior became GREEN. The attempted specification and generated production stub were fully reverted. This trace is admitted only as a non-training BUILD_ENVIRONMENT_FAILURE incident; numbered episode 0035 is not created.
